rationale_for_baseline_tier: An extensive (~35% TBSA) mixed-depth burn with a suspected inhalation injury (hoarse voice, singed facial hair, noisy breathing, throat burning), impending airway obstruction, developing hypovolaemic shock (dizziness, pallor, tachycardia, hypotension) and hypothermia, 15 minutes after a confined-space fire. This is an immediate, multi-system threat to life. T5 (call 000, emergency ambulance) is the only appropriate tier — the airway can occlude within minutes and definitive care (oxygen, airway control, IV fluid resuscitation, burns-unit transfer) is unavailable via telehealth. The on-scene clinician had already activated 000.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Worsening or increasingly noisy breathing, increasing hoarseness, throat swelling, or difficulty getting air in before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Stay on the line with 000 and report the change immediately; paramedics may need to secure the airway urgently
escalation_edges.rationale: Inhalation-injury airway oedema can progress to complete occlusion within minutes; escalating airway signs are the highest-priority deterioration.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If his breathing gets worse or noisier, or his throat swells more, tell 000 right away and keep him upright and calm — the paramedics may need to help him breathe.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Increasing dizziness, becoming pale/grey and clammy, or worsening drowsiness — deepening shock
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Keep him lying down and warm; report to 000 immediately
escalation_edges.rationale: Progressive burn shock requires the fluid resuscitation only paramedics/ED can provide; worsening perfusion signals imminent collapse.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he gets more dizzy, turns pale or grey, or becomes very drowsy, lay him down, keep him warm, and tell 000 straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Collapse or loss of consciousness / stops breathing normally
escalation_edges.rationale: Cardiorespiratory arrest is possible from airway occlusion or profound shock; dispatcher-guided CPR is the critical resource.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If he collapses or stops breathing normally, tell 000 immediately — they will talk you through starting CPR while the ambulance is on its way.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse is present, has secured the scene and is the one able to act on instructions
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Direct all carer-level actions to the spouse: keep him warm and still, do not apply water/ice/creams, do not attempt private transport, unlock the door for paramedics, and stay on the line with 000 to relay any deterioration.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance right now and tell them it is a major burn with breathing difficulty. Keep him warm and still — cover him with a clean, dry sheet or blanket. Do NOT put water, ice, creams, butter or any home remedy on the burns. Do not try to drive him to hospital yourselves. Watch his breathing closely and, if it worsens, tell 000 immediately. Unlock the front door and stay with him until the paramedics arrive.
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours a day — this is a life-threatening emergency and must not wait for clinic hours.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: The receiving burns unit will provide the discharge summary and ongoing care plan; the GP's role is post-acute follow-up, rehabilitation coordination and psychosocial support after transfer.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating an extensive burn with airway and shock signs as anything less than an emergency ambulance call.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Airway occludes or shock deepens at home; the patient dies before adequate help is arranged.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Advising self-transport to ED rather than a 000 ambulance for a patient with an evolving airway threat that could obstruct in a private car with no means to secure it.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Exact burn depth, TBSA, oxygenation and the true state of the airway cannot be measured over telehealth.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Escalate on the unambiguous emergency features (mechanism, extent, airway warning signs, shock signs) — uncertainty about precise severity raises, never lowers, the urgency.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Waiting to 'confirm' severity, or being reassured that the patient is still talking, and delaying the 000 call.
